# Amazon Redshift Connector - Full Documentation

# Amazon Redshift Connector 1.2 Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This example shows you how to use Anypoint Connector for Amazon Redshift (Amazon Redshift Connector)
to load data from Amazon S3 to Amazon Redshift.

## Create the Mule Project

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter `redshift-demo` as the name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.
3.  Open the `redshift-demo.xml` file in `src/main/mule` folder.
4.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
5.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `redshift` in the search field.
6.  Click **Amazon Redshift Connector** in **Available modules**.
7.  Click **Add**.
8.  Click **Finish**.
9.  Repeat the previous steps to also add the Amazon S3 Connector (search for `amazon s3`) and File
    Connector (search for `file`) to the Mule project.

## XML Code for This Example

Overwrite the contents of `redshift-demo.xml` file with the following XML snippet:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	  xmlns:s3="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s3"
	  xmlns:redshift="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redshift"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redshift http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redshift/current/mule-redshift.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s3 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s3/current/mule-s3.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">

	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" basePath="/">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<redshift:config name="Amazon_Redshift_Connector_Config" doc:name="Amazon Redshift Connector Config">
		<redshift:basic-connection
				clusterId="${redshift.cluster_id}"
				database="${redshift.database}"
				user="${redshift.user}"
				password="${redshift.password}"
				region="${redshift.region}"
				port="${redshift.port}"
		/>
	</redshift:config>

     <redshift:config name="Amazon_Redshift_Connector_Config_Fips" doc:name="Amazon Redshift Connector Config Fips">
		<redshift:iam-connection
                clusterId="${redshift.cluster_id}"
                database="${redshift.database}"
                user="${redshift.user}"
                accessKey="${redshift.password}"
                secretKey="${redshift.secret_key}" customStsEndpoint="${redshift.fips.sts}">
            <redshift:role arn="${redshift.role_arn}" />
		</redshift:iam-connection>
	</redshift:config>

	<s3:config name="Amazon_S3_Configuration" doc:name="Amazon S3 Configuration">
		<s3:basic-connection accessKey="${s3.access_key}" secretKey="${s3.secret_key}" region="${s3.region}"/>
	</s3:config>

	<file:config name="File_Config" doc:name="File Config">
		<file:connection workingDir="${mule.home}/apps/${app.name}/" />
	</file:config>

	<configuration-properties doc:name="Configuration properties" file="mule-artifact.properties" />

	<flow name="Create-Redshift-Table-S3-Bucket-S3-Object-Flow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/init"/>
		<set-variable value="username-bucket" doc:name="Bucket Name" variableName="bucket" />
		<redshift:execute-ddl doc:name="Execute DDL" config-ref="Amazon_Redshift_Connector_Config">
			<redshift:sql ><![CDATA[CREATE TABLE username (Username VARCHAR(50), Identifier INTEGER, First_Name VARCHAR(50), Last_Name VARCHAR(50));]]></redshift:sql>
		</redshift:execute-ddl>
		<s3:create-bucket doc:name="Create bucket" config-ref="Amazon_S3_Configuration" bucketName="#[vars.bucket]"/>
		<file:read doc:name="Read" config-ref="File_Config" path="username.csv"/>
		<s3:create-object doc:name="Create object" config-ref="Amazon_S3_Configuration" bucketName="#[vars.bucket]" key="username.csv" contentType="text/csv"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	success: true,
	redshiftTable: "username",
	s3bucket: vars.bucket,
	s3object: "username.csv"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>

	<flow name="Execute-Copy-Command-Flow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/execute"/>
		<set-variable value="#['s3://username-bucket/username.csv']" doc:name="Bucket Name" variableName="bucketNameEndpoint"/>
		<set-variable value="${copy.access_key}" doc:name="Access Key" variableName="access_key"/>
		<set-variable value="${copy.secret_key}" doc:name="Secret Key" variableName="secret_key"/>
		<redshift:execute-script doc:name="Execute script" config-ref="Amazon_Redshift_Connector_Config">
			<redshift:sql ><![CDATA[#["copy username from " ++ "'" ++ vars.bucketNameEndpoint ++ "'" ++ " access_key_id " ++ "'" ++ vars.access_key ++ "'" ++ " secret_access_key " ++ "'" ++ vars.secret_key ++ "'" ++ " delimiter ';' IGNOREHEADER 1 IGNOREBLANKLINES"]]]></redshift:sql>
		</redshift:execute-script>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	"success": true
}
]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>

	<flow name="Delete-Redshift-Table-S3-Bucket-Flow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/deleteAll"/>
		<set-variable value="username-bucket" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="bucketDelete"/>
		<redshift:execute-ddl doc:name="Execute DDL" config-ref="Amazon_Redshift_Connector_Config">
			<redshift:sql ><![CDATA[DROP TABLE username;]]></redshift:sql>
		</redshift:execute-ddl>
		<s3:delete-bucket doc:name="Delete bucket" config-ref="Amazon_S3_Configuration" force="true" bucketName='#[vars.bucketDelete]'/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	"success": true
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>

    <flow name="redshiftFipsDemoFlow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/redshift"/>
		<redshift:select doc:name="Select" config-ref="Amazon_Redshift_Connector_Config_Fips">
			<redshift:sql ><![CDATA[select * from <your-tableName>]]></redshift:sql>
		</redshift:select>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"  message="#[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Configure Global Elements

1.  Create a file named `mule-artifact.properties` in the `src/main/resources/` folder.
2.  Add the following properties in the `mule-artifact.properties` file and assign the correct
    values:

    s3.access_key= s3.secret_key= s3.region=

    redshift.cluster_id= redshift.region= redshift.port= redshift.database= redshift.user=
    redshift.password=

    copy.access_key= copy.secret_key=

    ***
    - You must have Amazon S3 credentials to create and delete buckets and to create an object.
    - Amazon Redshift credentials are required to establish a connection to the database.
    - This example is using a `COPY` command to load a table in parallel from a data file on Amazon
      S3.

      To use the `COPY` command, you must authenticate using your IAM user credentials with the
      correct policies attached to it, therefore, it is best to use the Amazon S3 Read only policy
      for the `copy.access_key` and `copy.secret_key` properties.

    - Alternatively, it is possible to use an IAM Role instead of the Access and Secret key pair.

      To do this, you must ensure the role has the correct policies attached to it. It is best to
      use the Amazon S3 Read only policy for this purpose.

    - To create an IAM role to allow your Redshift cluster to communicate with the Amazon S3 service
      on your behalf, follow the steps in this
      [Amazon Redshift tutorial](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/mgmt/authorizing-redshift-service.html).

3.  Open the Amazon Redshift configuration, scroll down, and configure the JDBC driver in the
    **Required libraries** section. In this example, choose the **Add recommended library** option.
    If this doesnât work, add the Amazon Maven repository to your `pom.xml` file and try again:

    ```xml
    <repositories>
    ...
        <repository>
            <id>redshift</id>
            <url>https://s3.amazonaws.com/redshift-maven-repository/release</url>
        </repository>
    </repositories>
    ```

The following images show the Amazon S3 and Amazon Redshift configurations:

![Amazon S3 configuration](_images/s3-configuration.png)

Figure 1. Amazon S3 Configuration

![Amazon Redshift Basic configuration](_images/redshift-configuration.png)

Figure 2. Amazon Redshift Basic Configuration

![Amazon Redshift IAM configuration](_images/redshift-configuration-iam.png)

Figure 3. Amazon Redshift IAM Configuration

![Amazon Redshift IAM configuration Advanced tab](_images/redshift-advanced-configuration.png)

Figure 4. Amazon Redshift IAM Configuration Advanced Tab

## Prepare the Data File

Prepare a data file to upload to Amazon S3. This data file will then be used as the data set in the
`COPY` command for the Amazon Redshift table.

1.  Create a file named `username.csv` in the `src/main/resources/` folder in your Mule project.
2.  Populate the `username.csv` file with the following sample data:

```csv
Username; Identifier;First name;Last name
booker12;9012;Rachel;Booker
grey07;2070;Laura;Grey
johnson81;4081;Craig;Johnson
jenkins46;9346;Mary;Jenkins
smith79;5079;Jamie;Smith
```

## Flows in This Example

The following screenshots show the Anypoint Studio app flows for this example:

- This flow creates the Amazon Redshift table, Amazon S3 bucket and object:

  ![Flow for creating the Redshift table Amazon S3 bucket and Amazon S3 object](_images/create-flow.png)

- This flow executes the `COPY` command, which leverages the Amazon Redshift massively parallel
  processing (MPP) architecture to load data in parallel from a file in an Amazon S3 bucket:

  ![Execute the Copy command flow](_images/execute-flow.png)

- This flow deletes the Amazon Redshift table and Amazon S3 bucket:

  ![Flow for deleting the Amazon Redshift table and Amazon S3 bucket](_images/delete-flow.png)

## Run the Example

1.  Right-click in the projectâs canvas and select **Run project redshift-demo**.
2.  Open `localhost:8081/init` in a web browser and wait until it returns a response containing
    `success:true` with the created table, bucket, and an object.
3.  Verify that the new Amazon S3 bucket `username-bucket` was created in your Amazon S3 instance.
4.  Verify that the new Redshift table `username` was created in your Amazon Redshift instance.
5.  Open `localhost:8081/execute` in a web browser and wait until it returns a response containing
    `success:true`.
6.  Verify that the `username` table contains data from the `username.csv` file you added to the
    Mule project in [Prepare the Data File](#prepare-data-file).
7.  Open `localhost:8081/delete` in a web browser and wait until it returns a response containing
    `success:true`.
8.  Verify that both the `username` Amazon Redshift table and `username-bucket` Amazon S3 bucket
    were deleted.

### See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon Redshift Connector 1.2 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Amazon Redshift (Amazon Redshift Connector) is a bulk-load connector that
enables you to connect to the Amazon Redshift database to perform inserts, deletes, updates, and to
execute SQL queries.

## Configurations

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Config_BasicConnection">Basic Connection</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Config_IamConnection">IAM Connection</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Connection Types

### Basic Connection

This connection uses database credentials for authentication.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Pooling Profile</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides a way to configure database connection pooling.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Column Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ColumnType">Column Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies non-standard column types.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transaction Isolation</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p></li><li><p>READ_COMMITTED</p></li><li><p>READ_UNCOMMITTED</p></li><li><p>REPEATABLE_READ</p></li><li><p>SERIALIZABLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_CONFIGURED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Transaction isolation level to set on the driver when connecting to the database.</p></td><td><p>NOT_CONFIGURED</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use XA Transactions</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether or not the created datasource must support XA transactions. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cluster Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Cluster identifier.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Region to use for authentication against the database.</p></td><td><p>us-east-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the port of the database.</p></td><td><p>5439</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Database</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Database name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Database user name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies a list of custom key-value connection properties to configure on the driver when connecting to the database.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password to use for authentication against the database.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### IAM Connection

This connection uses IAM credentials to retrieve temporary database credentials for authentication.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Pooling Profile</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides a way to configure database connection pooling.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Column Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ColumnType">Column Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies non-standard column types.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transaction Isolation</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p></li><li><p>READ_COMMITTED</p></li><li><p>READ_UNCOMMITTED</p></li><li><p>REPEATABLE_READ</p></li><li><p>SERIALIZABLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_CONFIGURED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Transaction isolation level to set on the driver when connecting to the database.</p></td><td><p>NOT_CONFIGURED</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use XA Transactions</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether or not the created datasource must support XA transactions. The default is false.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cluster Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Cluster identifier.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Region to use for authentication against the database.</p></td><td><p>us-east-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the port of the database.</p></td><td><p>5439</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Database</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Database name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Database user name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies a list of custom key-value connection properties to configure on the driver when connecting to the database.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Access key provided by Amazon for retrieving temporary database credentials.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Secret Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Secret key provided by Amazon for retrieving temporary database credentials.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Role</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Role">Role</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Role configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom STS Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets a custom STS endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC or FIPS endpoint. The custom STS endpoint might not work if <code>DefaultAWSCredentialsProviderChain</code> is used. Instead, use AWS environment variables such as <code>AWS_STS_REGIONAL_ENDPOINTS</code>, <code>AWS_REGION</code>, and <code>AWS_ENDPOINT_URL</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Sources

## On Table Row

`<redshift:listener>`

Selects from a table at a regular interval and generates one message per row that is obtained.
Optionally, watermark and ID columns can be provided. If a watermark column is provided, the values
taken from that column will be used to filter the contents of the next poll, so that only rows with
a greater watermark value are returned. If an ID column is provided, this component automatically
ensures that the same row is not picked twice by concurrent polls.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Table</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the table to select from.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Watermark Column</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the column to use for the watermark. Values taken from the watermark column will be used to filter the contents of the next poll, so that only rows with a greater watermark value are processed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id Column</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the column to use as the row ID. If provided, this component ensures that the same row is not processed twice by concurrent polls.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_BEGIN</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of beginning action that sources can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>NONE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transaction Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>LOCAL</p></li><li><p>XA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of transaction to create. Availability depends on Mule version.</p></td><td><p>LOCAL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

## Operations

- [Bulk Delete](#BulkDelete)
- [Bulk Insert](#BulkInsert)
- [Bulk Update](#BulkUpdate)
- [Delete](#Delete)
- [Execute DDL](#ExecuteDdl)
- [Execute Script](#ExecuteScript)
- [Insert](#Insert)
- [Query Single](#QuerySingle)
- [Select](#Select)
- [Stored Procedure](#StoredProcedure)
- [Update](#Update)

## Bulk Delete

`<redshift:bulk-delete>`

Enables the execution of one delete statement at various times using different parameter bindings.
This uses a single database statement, which has performance advantages compared to executing a
single delete operation at various times.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>A <code>java.util.List</code> of <code>java.util.Maps</code> in which every list item represents a row to be inserted. The map contains the parameter names as keys and the value the parameter is bound to.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameter Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Bulk Insert

`<redshift:bulk-insert>`

Enables the execution of one insert statement at various times using different parameter bindings.
This uses a single database statement, which has performance advantages compared to executing a
single update operation at various times.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>A java.util.List of java.util.Maps in which every list item represents a row to be inserted. The map contains the parameter names as keys and the value the parameter is bound to.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameter Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Bulk Update

`<redshift:bulk-update>`

Enables the execution of one update statement at various times using different parameter bindings.
This uses a single database statement, which has performance advantages compared to executing a
single update operation at various times.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>A java.util.List of java.util.Maps in which every list item represents a row to be inserted. The map contains the parameter names as keys and the value the parameter is bound to.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameter Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Delete

`<redshift:delete>`

Deletes data in a database.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameter Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Execute DDL

`<redshift:execute-ddl>`

Enables execution of DDL queries against a database.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Text of the SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Execute Script

`<redshift:execute-script>`

Executes a SQL script in a single database statement. The script is executed as provided by the
user, without any parameter binding.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Script Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Insert

`<redshift:insert>`

Inserts data into a database.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameter Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Generate Keys</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates when to make auto-generated keys available for retrieval.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Generated Keys Column Indexes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of column indexes that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Generated Keys Column Names</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of column names that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#StatementResult">Statement Result</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Query Single

`<redshift:query-single>`

Selects a single result from a database. If the specified SQL query returns more than one record,
only the first record is returned. Streaming is not used for this operation, so you must be careful
because all selected fields will be loaded to memory.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameter Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Select

`<redshift:select>`

Selects data from a database. Streaming is automatically applied to avoid performance and memory
issues that can be caused by preemptive consumption of results.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameter Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION

## Stored Procedure

`<redshift:stored-procedure>`

Invokes a Stored Procedure on the database. When the stored procedure returns one or more
java.sql.ResultSet instances, streaming is automatically applied to avoid performance and memory
issues that can be caused by preemptive consumption of results.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameter Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input - Output Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#OutputParameter">Output Parameter</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Generate Keys</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates when to make auto-generated keys available for retrieval.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Generated Keys Column Indexes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of column indexes that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Generated Keys Column Names</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of column names that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Update

`<redshift:update>`

Updates data in a database.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></td><td><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the minimum amount of time before a running statement is canceled. By default, no timeout is used.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Query Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit for the number of records to retrieve in a single page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the maximum number of rows to fetch from the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL Query Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SQL query to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameter Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ParameterType">Parameter Type</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Generate Keys</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates when to make auto-generated keys available for retrieval.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Generated Keys Column Indexes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of column indexes that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Generated Keys Column Names</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of column names that indicates which auto-generated keys to make available for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#StatementResult">Statement Result</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- REDSHIFT:BAD_SQL_SYNTAX
- REDSHIFT:CONNECTIVITY
- REDSHIFT:QUERY_EXECUTION
- REDSHIFT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Types

### Pooling Profile

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Pool Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Min Pool Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Acquire Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prepared Statement Cache Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Wait</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Wait Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Additional Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Column Type

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Class Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Role

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role to assume.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A unique identifier that might be required when you assume a role in another account. If the administrator of the account to which the role belongs provides an external ID, then provide that value in this field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Duration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The duration of the role session.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>3600</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Duration Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the Duration value.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Referred Policy Arns</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the IAM-managed policies to use as managed session policies. The policies must exist in the same account as the role.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tags</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A list of session tags that you want to pass. Each session tag consists of a key name and an associated value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance is allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering process-failed-message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use. If not set, the default is SHA-256.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SHA-256</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Identifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines which strategy is used to identify the messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property may only be set if <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> is false.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameter Type

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type Classifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TypeClassifier">Type Classifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Type Classifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BIT</p></li><li><p>TINYINT</p></li><li><p>SMALLINT</p></li><li><p>INTEGER</p></li><li><p>BIGINT</p></li><li><p>FLOAT</p></li><li><p>REAL</p></li><li><p>DOUBLE</p></li><li><p>NUMERIC</p></li><li><p>DECIMAL</p></li><li><p>CHAR</p></li><li><p>VARCHAR</p></li><li><p>LONGVARCHAR</p></li><li><p>DATE</p></li><li><p>TIME</p></li><li><p>TIMESTAMP</p></li><li><p>BINARY</p></li><li><p>VARBINARY</p></li><li><p>LONGVARBINARY</p></li><li><p>NULL</p></li><li><p>OTHER</p></li><li><p>JAVA_OBJECT</p></li><li><p>DISTINCT</p></li><li><p>STRUCT</p></li><li><p>ARRAY</p></li><li><p>BLOB</p></li><li><p>CLOB</p></li><li><p>REF</p></li><li><p>DATALINK</p></li><li><p>BOOLEAN</p></li><li><p>ROWID</p></li><li><p>NCHAR</p></li><li><p>NVARCHAR</p></li><li><p>LONGNVARCHAR</p></li><li><p>NCLOB</p></li><li><p>SQLXML</p></li><li><p>UNKNOWN</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Statement Result

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Affected Rows</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Generated Keys</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable
